name: sca_no_end_orphan
description: SCA orphan sessions — agents send Start + DataValues but never send End (simulating agent crash during FULL sync).
notes: Represents: 100 agents crash mid-FULL-sync (e.g., agent restart during SCA recovery).
Each agent sends 500 DataValues (500 x 4 KB = 2 MB per session in RocksDB).
Total RocksDB: 100 x 2 MB = 200 MB stuck on disk until session_timeout=1200s.
- m_agentSessions retains 100 sessions in RAM (GapSet + Context per session).
- RocksDB keys remain: {sessionId}_{0..499} for each agent.
- No EndAck is ever sent → agent-side PersistentQueue grows.
After timeout (20 min): inventory_sync should log 'Session N has timed out',
delete the RocksDB prefix, and remove from m_agentSessions.
→ RSS should drop and disk should shrink.
Production scenario: Network partition during SCA scan window.
Agent starts sending checks, network drops, agent restarts.
Manager holds orphan session data for 20 min.
If 1000 agents do this: 1000 x 2 MB = 2 GB stuck in RocksDB.
duration_sec=1500 (25 min) to observe the full timeout cycle.
